channel.title: Sqwod Daily
channel.description: The business of fitness, every weekday.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Sunday, 21 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Sunday, 21 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
⇄  Acquired
Peloton
 —
acquired connected Pilates startup Skōp to broaden its fitness portfolio beyond the bike
Connect the dots
Hardware gets smaller; the platforms get bigger. You're in the middle.
This week's news draws a clean line between two forces squeezing the independent operator: consolidation at the top and commoditization at the wrist. Peloton absorbing a connected Pilates startup, Oura shrinking a blood-pressure monitor onto your finger, an AI pin transcribing every coaching cue — the infrastructure of your business is being quietly rebuilt by companies that don't need your zip code to reach your clients.
The Rajnish Wellness numbers make the risk concrete: chasing growth while ignoring margin is how you hand the big platforms your lunch. Operators who win the next cycle won't out-tech Peloton — they'll out-margin them, out-retain them, and own the relationship the hardware can't replicate. The reformer room is worth fighting for, but only if the P&L survives long enough to fight.
The rundown
01
Peloton snaps up connected Pilates startup Skōp
Peloton has acquired Skōp, a connected Pilates innovator, as it moves to broaden its fitness portfolio beyond the bike. For studio operators and Pilates instructors, it's a signal that the big platforms are coming for your reformer room — worth watching closely.
Source: Yahoo! Finance Canada →
02
Revenue Up, Losses Heavier: Rajnish Wellness Q4 FY26 Tells Two Stories
Rajnish Wellness posted a revenue surge in Q4 FY26 — but heavy losses stole the headline. For studio operators, it's a sharp reminder that top-line growth means nothing if the bottom line is bleeding out on the gym floor.
Source: MarketsMojo →
03
Oura Ring 5 Is 40% Smaller and Now Tracks Blood Pressure
The latest Oura Ring just got a serious hardware upgrade — smaller form factor, plus blood pressure tracking that could make it a genuinely compelling tool for coaches who want clients monitoring recovery between sessions.
Source: The Gadgeteer →
04
This Tiny AI Pin Records Every Word You Say
The Plaud NotePin S is a wearable AI recorder that captures and transcribes conversations hands-free — meaning coaches can finally stop scrambling for a pen mid-session and actually focus on their client.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
Fitbod CEO says resistance training has been the core of fitness since day one — and it's not moving over for AI anytime soon. For operators, that's your reminder that no chatbot (Google Health Coach included) replaces a well-programmed lifting floor.
Source: TechRadar →
Stat
40%
reduction in Oura Ring 5 form factor size
A 40% smaller ring with blood pressure tracking lowers the barrier for client adoption — meaning recovery data your athletes used to ignore is now harder to take off, and harder for coaches to dismiss.
Sqwod recs
✓ Watch: Track how Peloton integrates Skōp — the pricing and instructor model they choose will telegraph exactly how they plan to compete with independent Pilates studios.
✓ Steal: Use a tool like the Plaud NotePin S or any hands-free transcription app during intake sessions this week — reviewing the transcript afterward will surface client language you can use directly in your marketing and programming.
✓ Try: Run a quick unit-economics check: calculate revenue per client per month against the direct cost to serve them. Rajnish Wellness is a cautionary tale, not a business plan.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Peloton, once the company that was going to kill the gym, has now acquired a Pilates startup to stay relevant. The arc of fitness tech hubris is truly something to behold.
Share Sqwod, get swag
Forward your link. Hit milestones, earn Sqwod gear — stickers, tee, hoodie. We count the referrals for you.
{{rp_personalized_text}}
Share your link →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free

Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 19 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: a million-dollar bet on smarter booking, a YouTuber-sized lesson in what you're actually selling, and a gym showing up where you'd least expect it. Let's go.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 19 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Presented by
Acme Recovery (sample)
Today's reps: a million-dollar bet on smarter booking, a YouTuber-sized lesson in what you're actually selling, and a gym showing up where you'd least expect it. Let's go.
— Sqwod
Money movement
▲  Raise
Vibefam
 $1M
Seed round to put AI + fintech inside studio booking and payments. →
▲  Raise
Recovery-tech startup (sample)
 €3.5M
Illustrative row — in production this auto-fills from the week's real raises.
⇄  Acquired
Boutique studio group (sample)
 undisclosed
Illustrative — auto-filled from ingested M&A news.
Connect the dots
The gym is leaving the building
Three stories, one thread. Vibefam raised $1M to make booking software think; Athlean-X turned an audience into the whole business; a luxury marina is bolting a gym onto its docks. None of these is really about a room with weights in it.
The value is migrating off the floor — into software, audiences, and amenities. For a coach or studio owner that's uncomfortable but freeing: your four walls are your least defensible asset. Your data, your audience, and the relationship you own are what compound. Spend your next euro accordingly.
The rundown
01
Vibefam bags $1M to make your booking app actually think
The seed round is a bet that AI and fintech eat scheduling next. Translation for studio owners: the back office is about to get a lot smarter — and the operators still doing it by hand, a lot lonelier.
Source: BriefGlance →
02
Athlean-X proves the real product was the audience all along
Jeff Cavaliere built a fitness empire on YouTube reps, not retail shelves. The lesson for coaches: your content isn't marketing for the business — increasingly, it is the business.
Source: AD HOC NEWS →
03
A superyacht marina is adding a gym. Yes, really.
When luxury real estate treats fitness as a must-have amenity, that's a tell: 'where the people are' now comes with a squat rack. Operators, your next location might not look like a gym at all.
Source: Marine Business News →
Policy watch
Germany tightens influencer-ad labeling — coaches, this is you
If you post sponsored training content, UWG rules on clear 'Werbung/Anzeige' labeling apply. Unlabeled promos are the fastest way to earn a warning letter (Abmahnung). Label every paid post, every time.
Source →
Stat
1 in 3
new members gone by March (sample)
Illustrative figure — in production this pulls from your Statista/Forge data. Retention, not acquisition, is where studio profit actually hides. →
Sponsored · Presented by Acme Recovery (sample)
Clean electrolytes built for two-a-days — no sugar, no chalk, no nonsense. Coaches get 30% off team orders.
Get 30% off →
Sqwod recs
✓ Read: Our teardown of a 200-member studio's real P&L.
✓ Steal: The 3-text win-back sequence that reactivates lapsed members.
✓ Try: A 10-minute weekly 'money movement' scan so deals never blindside you.
✓ Gear: Oura Ring Gen3 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? Take the 30-second reaction test — the same psychomotor vigilance task sleep scientists use to measure alertness and fatigue.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
This week's main character: the lifter who racked one safety clip, skipped the other, and let physics finish the set. Gravity remains undefeated — and yes, there's video. →
Share Sqwod, get swag
Forward your link. Hit milestones, earn Sqwod gear — stickers, tee, hoodie. We count the referrals for you.
{{rp_personalized_text}}
Share your link →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 17 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 17 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Wearables cross €84bn — and rings are the fastest-growing slice
The category grows ~9% a year, but smart rings are up 31% YoY. For coaches, that means recovery data is going mainstream — and your clients are already wearing it.
Source: Wearables →
02
The 8.5% that decides if your studio survives
Average boutique churn sits near 8.5%/month and acquiring a member costs ~6x retaining one. The fix isn't more ads — it's the first 30 days. Here's the onboarding math.
Source: Boutique studios →
03
Recovery is eating the supplement aisle
Consumer recovery spend is up 22% and 41% now track sleep. The story of 2026 isn't harder workouts — it's the business of resting better.
Source: Recovery economy →
Sqwod recs
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod, get swag

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 16 June 2026
item.description: Investors double down on fitness, recovery funding surges, AI coaching scales.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 16 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Investors double down on fitness, recovery funding surges, AI coaching scales.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Investors double down on fitness
Capital is rotating back into the category after a quiet stretch. Funded competitors mean faster-moving markets — and richer exit options for operators.
02
Recovery funding surges
Money is chasing rest as hard as it once chased reps. Recovery is becoming its own profit center, not a wellness afterthought.
03
AI coaching scales
Automated programming and check-ins are getting good enough to handle the bottom of your client ladder. Use it to free your time for the high-touch tier that actually pays.
Share Sqwod, get swag
Forward your link. Hit milestones, earn Sqwod gear — stickers, tee, hoodie. We count the referrals for you.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 12 June 2026
item.description: Studio M&A heats up, wearables add UV tracking, menopause fitness goes mainstream.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 12 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Studio M&A heats up, wearables add UV tracking, menopause fitness goes mainstream.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Studio M&A heats up
Consolidation is accelerating as larger groups roll up independents. Know your numbers — you're either an acquirer, a target, or roadkill.
02
Wearables add UV tracking
Sensors keep expanding into everyday health metrics, deepening the data clients bring you. The coach's edge is interpretation, not collection.
03
Menopause fitness goes mainstream
A once-taboo market is getting purpose-built programming and real budgets. Specializing here is one of the clearest white-space plays in fitness right now.
Share Sqwod, get swag

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 11 June 2026
item.description: A big-box CEO exits, a new hydration brand launches, alcohol's risks resurface.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 11 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
A big-box CEO exits, a new hydration brand launches, alcohol's risks resurface.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
A big-box CEO exits
Leadership churn at a fitness giant is a tell that the mass-membership model is under strain. Boutique operators should read it as an opening, not just gossip.
02
A new hydration brand launches
Another entrant in the electrolyte gold rush shows how cheap a beverage is to launch — and how crowded the shelf has become. Differentiation now lives in distribution, not formula.
03
Alcohol's risks resurface
Fresh health warnings keep nudging clients toward sober-curious habits. Coaches who frame training as the better high have the cultural wind at their back.
Share Sqwod, get swag

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 10 June 2026
item.description: Supplements add creatine, recovery tech expands, creator brands scale up.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 10 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Supplements add creatine, recovery tech expands, creator brands scale up.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Supplements add creatine
Creatine is escaping the bro-science aisle into mainstream wellness SKUs. An easy, evidence-backed upsell for coaches who'd rather sell trust than hype.
02
Recovery tech expands
Saunas, cold plunges and compression are becoming standalone revenue lines, not perks. Increasingly the recovery room can out-earn the weight room.
03
Creator brands scale up
Fitness creators are turning audiences into product lines at speed. The lesson for operators: your following is inventory you haven't monetized yet.
Share Sqwod, get swag

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 09 June 2026
item.description: Pilates keeps winning, athletes reject soda, women's health raises $215M.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 09 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Pilates keeps winning, athletes reject soda, women's health raises $215M.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Pilates keeps winning
Low-impact, high-margin and endlessly shareable, Pilates keeps stealing share from traditional gyms. If you don't offer a version of it, your churned members already found one.
02
Athletes reject soda
Elite athletes ditching sugary drinks is handing functional-beverage brands their marketing script. Expect the trickle-down to reshape what sells at your front desk.
03
Women's health raises $215M
Capital is finally flowing into a long-ignored market. The studios and apps that design for women specifically — not 'shrink it and pink it' — will own the upside.
Share Sqwod, get swag
Forward your link. Hit milestones, earn Sqwod gear — stickers, tee, hoodie. We count the referrals for you.
{{rp_personalized_text}}

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 05 June 2026
item.description: Longevity goes commercial, non-alc doubles down, food-as-medicine breaks through.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 05 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Longevity goes commercial, non-alc doubles down, food-as-medicine breaks through.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Longevity goes commercial
What was a biohacker niche is turning into shelves, clinics and memberships. Early movers are productizing 'live longer' before the mainstream catches on.
02
Non-alc doubles down
Zero-proof is no longer a January gimmick — brands are building year-round businesses. For studios, sober social events are a cheap, on-brand retention play.
03
Food-as-medicine breaks through
Insurers and employers are starting to pay for nutrition as treatment. Coaches who can speak the clinical language plug into budgets that dwarf consumer wallets.
Share Sqwod, get swag

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 04 June 2026
item.description: A $435M longevity raise, immersive fitness returns, personalized health expands.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 04 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
A $435M longevity raise, immersive fitness returns, personalized health expands.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
A $435M longevity raise
The biggest longevity bet yet says real money is chasing healthspan, not just lifespan. Coaches who sell 'add good years to your life' now have a funded category at their back.
02
Immersive fitness returns
Screen-led classes and VR workouts are back as operators hunt for experiences clients can't replicate at home. The differentiator is no longer the equipment — it's the room.
03
Personalized health expands
From bloodwork to wearables, 'built for you' is becoming table stakes. Generic, one-size programs are getting harder to charge premium prices for.
Share Sqwod, get swag
